jobs.title: Nuc Innovation &Technology Strategic Adv
jobs.description: Dominion Energy is committed to providing reliable, affordable, and increasingly clean energy that powers our customers every day. If you want to work for a purpose-driven company that values safety and collaboration, we’re looking for you. You won’t just find a job here; you’ll find your career. Review the position below and apply today. Military service members and veterans with a rank of E-5 or higher plus appropriate equivalent combination of education and years of experience as outlined below will be considered for this opportunity. At this time, Dominion Energy cannot transfer or sponsor a work visa or employment authorization for this position. Job Summary Nuclear Power has played an important role in meeting the world’s electrical energy needs for 50+ years. And with power demand rising dramatically in the decades ahead, Nuclear Power will be indispensable in meeting the concurrent challenges of Reliability and Affordability while also limiting air pollution. For engineers and innovators, very few industries can match Nuclear Power for its ability to offer dynamic technical challenges with long-term job security. This will be particularly true for those pathfinders who help companies like Dominion Energy identify and implement new technologies, tools, and business practices which will improve safety, reliability, and overall power production. The Nuclear Innovation and Technology Strategic Advisor (“Advisor”) is recognized as a high-level strategic expert and advisor within Dominion Energy’s Nuclear division who is responsible for exploring and analyzing innovational advances. This work will embrace some of the most complex IT-related problems that will impact the direction of the business. The Advisor will proactively identify projects and then develop business, scope, risk, and prioritization case studies to include benchmarking studies. Once developed, they will drive awareness of potential new solutions across Nuclear’s senior leadership team. Further, the Advisor will lead, influence or directly manage implementation of new initiatives with a key focus on eliminating duplication of efforts between teams. For example, they will lead the collaboration between Nuclear and the IT division on AI initiatives. The Advisor identifies opportunities to streamline processes and partnerships across Dominion Energy and the Nuclear division which will enhance business value. They will define required resources, assemble project teams, assign individual responsibilities, and monitor the work of team members. In leading key initiatives, the Advisor will be responsible for monitoring project progress and results. This work will include the development of thorough project plans to ensure deadlines and budgets are met. They will also analyze projects to identify lessons that can be leveraged to improve future initiatives. The Advisor will provide overall guidance and advisement to business unit executive leadership on new solutions which can have significant impact on the Nuclear division’s long-term strategies. In all aspects of their role, the Advisor will have to effectively navigate business and regulatory priorities/barriers. Finally, the Advisor will be responsible for establishing and nurturing strategic internal and external business partnerships. This includes representing Dominion Energy in industry innovation initiatives (i.e. EPRI, NEI, Nuclear Business Information Council, INPO Data Science Working Group, and other specific industry groups/projects). In all aspects of their role, the Advisor must communicate with clarity and precision. They will need to effectively build trust, rapport and influence with a wide variety of stakeholders and partners. They must never lose sight of Dominion Energy’s high standards for excellence as it relates to reliability, affordability, and increasing clean energy solutions.Required Knowledge, Skills, Abilities & Experience To be qualified for the Nuclear Innovation and Technology Strategic Advisor, one must meet the following requirements: 10+ years of relevant experience in Nuclear Information Technology (IT) or Engineering; Expert in the adaptation to, and the application of, rapidly changing technology to meet business needs; Proven knowledge of emerging energy technologies; Strong interpersonal communication skill Proven ability to influence others outside their own organization to accomplish objectives; Action-oriented with demonstrated experience in driving multi-disciplinary teams to reach conclusions and to drive decision-making to go/no-go conclusions; Understanding of the legal framework and process for the protection of intellectual property rights; Qualitative, quantitative, and research skills, including fundamental understanding of applied business and finance concepts; Strong organizational, project management, written and oral communication skills and the ability to work independently; Strong team-building skills with demonstrated skill and ability to effectively lead teams to achieve project goals and milestones; Excellent negotiation skills; Demonstrated planning skills to include project coordination and the ability to manage multiple deadlines; Demonstrated competency in developing effective solutions to business problems; Demonstrated ability to analyze problems and to think logically Education Requirements Degree or an equivalent combination of education and demonstrated related experience may be accepted in lieu of preferred level of education. The preferred candidate will possess a bachelor’s degree in engineering, Information Technology, Computer Science, or closely related field. Other disciplines may be accepted in lieu of the preferred discipline(s) listed above. jobs.title: Intern-Nuclear Nondestructive Examination (NDE)
jobs.description: Dominion Energy is committed to providing reliable, affordable, and increasingly clean energy that powers our customers every day. If you want to work for a purpose-driven company that values safety and collaboration, we’re looking for you. You won’t just find a job here; you’ll find your career. Review the position below and apply today. At this time, Dominion Energy cannot transfer or sponsor a work visa or employment authorization for this position. Job Summary We are currently seeking an intern, preferably for the summer and early fall 2026, in the Corporate Nuclear NDE group located at Dominion Energy's Innsbrook Technical Center in Glen Allen, VA. The fall portion of this internship would involve approximately three to four weeks of hands-on refueling outage experience at one of our nuclear stations. While this is the preferred timeframe for the internship, it is possible that alternate 2026 schedules may be accommodated if that schedule meets the mutual benefit of the intern and Dominion Energy. Position Overview The Corporate Nuclear Engineering NDE (Nondestructive Examination) group has responsibility for developing, maintaining, and implementing the fleet NDE Program to support the Dominion Energy nuclear plants in Virginia, Connecticut, and South Carolina. The fleet NDE Program provides a standardized method for establishing fleet NDE procedures, guidelines, processes, and direction to ensure a consistent approach for implementation of NDE at each site. The NDE Program includes NDE performed in support of ASME Section XI Preservice and Inservice Inspection, balance of plant (non-Section XI related), Flow Accelerated Corrosion, License Renewal, Subsequent License Renewal , engineering and maintenance requested examinations. A intern in NDE would assist the corporate NDE staff perform nondestructive examinations of power plant welds, components, and equipment during normal plant operation and outage situations for the Dominion Energy nuclear fleet. Travel to nuclear stations within the fleet is required to provide NDE support as needed..Required Knowledge, Skills, Abilities & Experience The most qualified candidate would have the skills and knowledge to properly perform specific calibrations, specific tests, and be able to follow approved nondestructive testing procedures under the direct guidance or supervision of an NDE Level II or NDE Level III examiner. The most qualified candidate will have had some exposure to performing nondestructive examinations in the Magnetic Particle (MT), Liquid Penetrant (PT), and Ultrasonic (UT) NDE methods. The most qualified candidate would demonstrate a strong ability to rapidly grasp new technical concepts and work either independently or as a group to meet challenging goals. The most qualified candidate would demonstrate an ability to collaborate effectively with a team and would demonstrate strong communication skills. Required Knowledge, Skills, Abilities & Experience Professional orientation and effective interpersonal skills. Effective communication skills in both written and oral forms. Ability to work in teams or independently under own initiative. Ability to rapidly learn new concepts and maintain cognizance and control of multiple project tasks. Problem-solving skills, including demonstrated ability to define problems, collect information, evaluate data, draw valid conclusions, and develop solutions. Ability to effectively operate a personal computer and standard Microsoft applications. Professional orientation and effective interpersonal skills; effective communication skills in both written and oral forms; ability to work in teams or independently under own initiative. Education Requirements Working toward an associate in applied science degree in Nondestructive Examination with a graduation date from fall 2026 to spring 2027 As a minimum, completed training, equivalent to a SNT-TC-1A or CP-189 Level I examiner, in the PT, MT, and UT testing methods. GPA of 3.0 or higher. jobs.title: Mgr Nuclear Projects Corp (CFAM)
jobs.description: Dominion Energy is committed to providing reliable, affordable, and increasingly clean energy that powers our customers every day. At this time, Dominion Energy cannot transfer or sponsor a work visa or employment authorization for this position. Job Summary The continued success of Dominion Energy’s Nuclear division requires the efforts and expertise of a wide variety of professionals. It also demands exceptional planning and logistical coordination. To help orchestrate these teams and resources, the Nuclear Division is actively looking to hire a Manager Nuclear Projects– Corporate (CFAM). The Manager will be responsible for providing strategic oversight, governance and leadership to drive the nuclear business unit performance in the assigned functional area and is accountable for performance outcomes that include project implementation and oversight across the nuclear business unit. They will systematically oversee and routinely evaluate project performance through review and analysis of data resulting in recommendations to better integrate plant processes. Routine responsibilities also include: frequent site visits and participation in station management review meetings representing Dominion Energy with INPO and other industry groups that evaluate Dominion Energy performance and provide industry leadership in support of industry performance improvement. The Manager will serve as the lead for the Fleet Peer Group Governance and Oversight team supporting the Project Management Peer Group. Further, the Manager will be responsible for the review, analysis and integration of plant processes, procedures, project controls protocols and practices, and overseeing the development of a consistent and integrated standard plant administrative process/procedure platform. They will ensure the inclusion of industry best practices in peer group products. The Manager is directly responsible for leading station and corporate project leaders in the governance of fleet projects implementation and controls from a position of credibility and influence to achieve industry best practices in projects across the Dominion Energy nuclear fleet. They will work closely with all levels of the Peer Group process, maintain frequent communication with the Peer Group Executive Leader, Chairperson, and Peer Group members. The Manager carries significant influence and works directly with the peer group executive leader and chair to establish fleet-wide actions and priorities. The Manager is expected to stay abreast of industry best performance and obtains useful information for their Peer Group. Continually assesses industry initiatives and standards that are constantly evolving, and research internal assessments, reports, and observations to identify site-specific deficiencies for fleet-wide shortfall. Assesses INPO evaluations and NRC reports and researches other industry activities (technical, safety, organizational, etc.) for potential opportunities to operate more safely, reliably and efficiently. Identifies benchmarking and self-assessment opportunities and ensures personnel take advantage of them in support of NBU and Peer Group priorities. The Manager will be expected to work across other discipline CFAMs to ensure project implementation standards and protocols are supported by all disciplines to ensure successful outcomes in project execution. In all aspects of their role, the Manager will need to establish and nurture productive partnerships across a variety of teams and functions. Success will depend upon their ability to effectively collaborate with stakeholders and influence positive action to advance essential work. The Manager must excel at managing schedules, identifying blinds spots, managing risk, clarifying issues, and developing solutions. They must demonstrate an inquisitive mind to understand the details essential to ultimate success. Required Knowledge, Skills, Abilities & Experience The knowledge, skills, abilities and experience required for entry into this job include the following: 8+ years of supervisory or management level experience in project implementation in power generation industry. jobs.title: Manager-Radiological Protection & Chemistry
jobs.description: Dominion Energy is committed to providing reliable, affordable, and increasingly clean energy that powers our customers every day. If you want to work for a purpose-driven company that values safety and collaboration, we’re looking for you. You won’t just find a job here; you’ll find your career. Review the position below and apply today. Military service members and veterans with a rank of E-5 or higher plus appropriate equivalent combination of education and years of experience as outlined below will be considered for this opportunity. Job Summary The Radiological Protection & Chemistry team at Millstone Power Station plays an essential role in the safe operation of the site by protecting people and their environment from potential hazards. Leading the team to achieve the highest levels operational performance is the Manager – Radiological Protection & Chemistry (“Manager”). As the department senior leader, the Manager will be responsible for a wide variety of department activities to include: Senior-level supervision of overall Radiological Protection programs, Radiation Protection & Chemistry controls, and radioactive waste management; Planning and direction of the team’s deliverables and priorities; Ensuring that individual team members are properly trained and fully qualified to execute their assignment; Ensuring the department is properly manned and equipped to execute assigned work; Evaluating the effectiveness of radiological protection and chemistry program elements, identifying weaknesses and taking necessary actions to improve performance; Analyzing trends in radiological/chemical work performance to identity adverse trends and then developing necessary action plans to improve performance; Developing and implementing policy changes as appropriate; Monitoring the effectiveness of chemistry controls used to protect vital equipment; Managing radioactive waste processing and disposal programs to minimize station effluent releases and land burial quantities; Identifying and reviewing causes, concerns, and corrective actions for radiological and chemical incidents to ensure appropriate actions are taken to prevent recurrence Developing and managing department budgets and operational goals. Further, the Manager role may be selected to act as the site Radiation Protection Manager (RPM) as described in the Nuclear Facility Quality Assurance Program Description (DOM-QA-1) section 1.2. In all aspects of their role, the Manager is expected to establish and grow effective partnerships with all other departments at Millstone Power Station. They will also be expected to identify strategic risks that can result from changes in the regulatory environment. They must maintain a laser focus on executing essential daily action items while nurturing a sustainable team culture dedicated to constant improvement. Required Knowledge, Skills, Abilities & Experience To be qualified for the Manager-Radiological Protection & Chemistry, one must meet the following required knowledge, skills, abilities and experiences: 8+ years of relevant experience related to radiological protection and/or chemistry 3+ years of nuclear power plant experience 1+ years of supervisor or management experience Completion of DOM-QA-1, "Nuclear Facility Quality Assurance Program Description", section 2.5 and Appendix C. Demonstrated proficiency in leading and directing the implementation of the Radiation Protection program Strong communications with other managers as well as direct reports. Proven ability to provide clear direction and expectations Demonstrated presentation skills to large groups of employees Strong interpersonal skills to work with multiple groups; ability to motivate and lead an organization with limited direction. Proven ability to foster teamwork at the manager and worker level by communicating needs and expected outcomes Ability to realign resources to meet the most critical needs tied to improve overall performance. Demonstrated capability to follow up on performance shortfalls and get organization to respond. – Broad knowledge of programs and functions across nuclear power stations Perform general supervisory administrative duties including, but not limited to, budget control, employee performance reviews, coaching, and correcting. Preferred Candidates will also possess: National Registry of Radiation Protection Technologists (NRRPT) or Certified Health Physicist (CHP) preferred Holds or has held a Senior Reactor Operator (SRO) license Education Requirements Required Degree: (equivalency not acceptable) Bachelor Preferred Disciplines: Engineering, Radiation Science, Science Other related physical science degree may be substituted for the preferred discipline(s) listed above.Licenses, Certifications, or Quals Description Preferred: National Registry of Radiation Protection Technologists (NRRPT) or Certified Health Physicist (CHP). ANSI/ANS-3.1-2014, Section 4.3.3, NRC Regulatory Guide 1.8, Revision 4, Staff Regulatory Guidance 1.3 Education requirements: Baccalaureate in related physical science or engineering and specific formal training in radiation protection. Minimum experience for the position: Related experience which shall include 5 yrs; Nuclear power plant experience to include 3 yrs; Supervisory or management experience to include 1 yr. Special requirements: 1) During the years of nuclear power plant experience, the individual shall have participated in supervisory or management activities at a nuclear power plant during the following periods: a) 1 month of routine refueling outage, b) 2 months of operation above 20% power; 2) The 3 years of nuclear power plant experience should at least be at a level requiring policy, planning and decision making related to the programmatic aspects of the radiation protection program as a whole; 3) The individual filling the radiation protection manager responsibilities for the station shall have 3 years of experience in two or more functional areas of radiological protection, which includes as low as reasonably achievable, bioassay/whole body, decontamination, dosimetry, radiochemistry, radiological environmental monitoring program/radiological effluent technical specification, Radiation Protection Instrumentation, Radiation Protection operations, or radioactive waste. Substation Engineering Job Summary Dominion Energy Virginia is offering an exciting opportunity for an engineer or engineering technical specialist to join our Electric Transmission - Substation Engineering - Physical Design department. In this role, you’ll play a crucial role in shaping the future of our high-voltage electric grid system, which operates from 69kV to 500kV. This oppurtunity will be involved with greenfield and brownfield projects, data center related projects, and renewable generation interconnect projects, to name a few. The successful candidate will be able to satisfy the following: Motivated and Engaged Engineer: We’re seeking someone who is passionate about their work, eager to innovate, and ready to contribute to our dynamic team. Responsibilities: You’ll be involved in the development detailed design and construction review. Your focus will be on the physical design of electrical substations, both for new construction and upgrades to existing facilities. Engineering Expertise: You’ll apply your expertise in substation physical design, including creating and maintaining standard drawings for electrical, mechanical, and control systems. This includes developing engineering packages, performing calculations, conducting analysis, and ensuring compliance with industry standards. Independence and Adaptability: You’ll exercise independent judgment, evaluating and selecting engineering techniques and criteria. You’ll also adapt to moderately complex challenges, making necessary modifications to achieve optimal results. Document Review and Collaboration: You’ll prepare and review critical substation documents, such as one-line diagrams, general arrangements, elevations, conduit and foundation plans, cable schedules, ground grid plans, and bills of materials. Collaboration with other disciplines, including grading plan development, steel structures, and bus design, will be essential. Site Visits and Modeling: Engage in site visits to support construction activities and asset owners. Additionally, experience with modeling tools (preferably Microstation and/or AutoCAD) will be advantageous. jobs.title: Senior Nuclear Equipment Operator - RBS
jobs.description: Job Title: Senior Nuclear Equipment Operator - RBS Work Place Flexibility: Onsite Legal Entity: Entergy Operations, Inc.-EOI **This requsition will fill 6 vacancies** JOB QUALIFICATIONS: Under directive supervision, must be able to assume responsibility for the operation of the equipment included in a central controlled turbo‑generator‑nuclear boiler system with its associated auxiliaries and functions during a specific shift, and be qualified to do the following and related work as assigned: Place in service, or remove from service, and operate in a safe manner, turbo‑generator and nuclear boiler systems and related auxiliary equipment such as valves, traps, regulators, desuperheaters, heaters, cooling towers, heat exchangers, and related piping systems containing water, steam, air, and other fluids or gases, station service electric circuits, oil circuit breakers, air circuit breakers, disconnecting switches, transformers, storage batteries, motor generator sets, indicating and recording instruments, reactor feed and heater drain pump, etc. Check bearing temperatures and equipment for proper operation. Record operating data on log sheets as required. Perform necessary housekeeping as per applicable Nuclear Regulatory Commission endorsed regulations and standards. Comply with established procedures and with rules of Code of Federal Regulations, Nuclear Regulatory Commission regulatory guides, National Standards and other regulatory agencies. Participate and maintain satisfactory progress in all aspects of training and requalification programs. EXPERIENCE AND SPECIAL QUALIFICATIONS: Understand principles of operation, function, and construction of nuclear boilers, turbo‑generators, temperature, pressure and level regulators, their controls and auxiliary equipment. Have practical knowledge of the principles of operation of rotating electrical equipment and appurtenant apparatus. Have a complete mental picture and be able to sketch from memory a flow diagram of main piping systems. Have ability to understand and perform semi‑routine operations of considerable complexity and variety where standard methods of procedure are available. Know Company's safety rules, and be able to administer all approved methods of resuscitation, and be able to render practical first aid. Know the location of, and be able to operate, all of the firefighting equipment. Know what kind of equipment to use on electrical, oil, and other fires. Achieve satisfactory results in all applicable portions of the Nuclear Plant Training program and obtain/maintain a valid reactor operator license issued by the Nuclear Regulatory Commission or applicable regulatory agency. **Desired: Technical Degree such as: Mechanical , Electrical , Civil Engineering or similar preferred** PHYSICAL QUALIFICATIONS: Pass physical examination for Nuclear Regulatory Commission Regulation 396, "Certificate of Physical Exam" and Guide 1.134, "Medical Evaluation of Nuclear Power Plant Personnel Requiring Operator Licenses". PERSONAL QUALIFICATIONS: Be particularly alert in observing hazards and avoiding accidents. Be willing to attend and take part in safety meetings. Be willing to work under unusual surroundings, as well as the normal working conditions pertaining to this job. Be willing to work irregular or extra hours as required. Be able to receive and transmit orders and instructions in such a manner as to inspire respect and confidence of both superiors and subordinates. Be especially neat, clean and orderly in work and person. Understand importance of always rendering and maintaining first class, courteous service to all customers. Be willing to attend and meet all annual requalification training requirements in all areas of operation. EMPLOYMENT TESTING: Successful completion of the EEI POSS test. **Candidates selected to take the POSS Test will be invited via e-mail. You may need to check your spam email. ***If you are POSS Recommended, please note it on your resume. jobs.description: Posting End Date: Work Place Flexibility: Onsite Legal Entity: Entergy Operations, Inc.-EOI Position may be filled at Echelon Nuclear in Jackson, MS or River Bend Nuclear Station in St. Francisville, LA. jobs.title: Nuclear Auxiliary Operator O/1
jobs.description: Job Title: Nuclear Auxiliary Operator O/1 Work Place Flexibility: Onsite Legal Entity: Entergy Operations, Inc.-EOI * This posting is intended to fill multiple vacancies. * JOB SUMMARY/PURPOSE: The Nuclear Auxiliary Operator performs operations, routine tests, incidental maintenance, electrical switching, and other activities on equipment necessary to ensure continuity of power generation in accordance with administrative, procedural, and regulatory requirements. Nuclear Auxiliary Operators perform periodic inspections of assigned equipment and spaces to identify problems, defects, and failures which degrade plant integrity and reliability. Nuclear Auxiliary Operators maintain familiarity with the conditions and limitations of equipment operation, out of specification conditions, logs, and surveillances. JOB DUTIES/RESPONSIBILITIES: Regular and reliable attendance in support of a 12 hour rotating shift schedule, including working weekends, holidays, or call-ins to maintain NRC minimum staffing requirements for position. Monitor, inspect and adjust equipment for proper operation. Collect and trend equipment and system operating parameters. Identify equipment problems, defects, and failures which degrade plant integrity and reliability. Analyze component and system performance trends to identify challenges to its operation. Report any abnormal plant or equipment conditions to the control room and take appropriate action to mitigate the consequences. Perform routine servicing of equipment including lubrication, wiping of residual oil and ensure fluid leakage is properly contained and managed. Recommend changes in plant procedures, personnel, operation, and equipment status to maximize plant and personnel efficiency. Perform technical reviews of procedures and other documents. Utilize conservative decision making principles and human performance behaviors. Utilize the station's corrective action programs to address issues and opportunities for improvement. Assist other site departments with test specifications and performance. Participate in training and personnel development opportunities. Serve as a member of the Emergency Response Organization. Perform the initial actions required in response to an off-normal condition and perform follow up actions as directed. Perform tasks related to natural emergency response, including operation of debris removal equipment. As a member of the site Fire Brigade, perform firefighting, rescue and chemical spill response activities. All other duties as assigned. MINIMUM REQUIREMENTS:Minimum Education: High School diploma or equivalent. Classroom courses in basic algebra and physics. Desired: B.S. degree in Engineering, Science, Math, or similar. Minimum Experience: 0 - 2 years operations experience or commercial nuclear power operations experience or a related vocational, trade school, apprentice program, or equivalent military training. Experience in nuclear power plant operations. Desired: Military nuclear experience. Minimum Knowledge, Skills and Abilities: Ability to comprehend and apply mechanical, electrical, and nuclear engineering principles to operation of a power plant. Attend classroom training and pass written examinations to achieve and maintain qualifications. Ability to Communicate technical information, concepts, and ideas verbally and in writing; Be able to read prints. Any Certificates, Licenses, etc.: Successful Completion of EEI POSS test with a "Recommended" status. Candidates selected to EEI POSS test, will receive testing invite via email. *** MONITOR YOUR JUNK/SPAM FOLDER. Physical/Mental Demands and Working Conditions: Must be able to walk long distances and be on feet for several hours at a time in outdoor and industrial environments (such as areas with high heat and humidity); Must be able to perform routine activities associated with an industrial environment, such as Climbing stairs, ladders, scaffolding; wearing a body harness; bending, stooping, twisting, crawling, kneeling, and working in tight spaces; lifting, pushing, pulling, /carrying up to 50lbs. Must be able to wear a respirator. Must be able to be a Radiation Worker Applicant will be required to successfully complete the following to be considered for the position: EEI - Plant Operations System Selection (POSS) test Physical examinations to support operator, respirator, and fire brigade qualification Requisites to obtain unescorted access to the power plant, which include psychological examination, drug screen and security background check. #LI-CC1Primary Location: Arkansas-Russellville Arkansas : Russellville Job Function: Nuclear FLSA Status: Nonexempt Relocation Option: Auxiliary Operators Nuclear Union description/code: ARKANSAS BARG. -- NUCLEAR Number of Openings: 15 Req ID: 122211 Travel Percentage:Up to 25% An Equal Opportunity Employer, Minority/Female/Disability/Vets. jobs.title: Control Room Supervisor
jobs.description: Job Title: Control Room Supervisor Work Place Flexibility: Onsite Legal Entity: Entergy Operations, Inc.-EOI This posting will fill 4 vacancies.* Relocation assistance is provided. **Selected candidates will move into the Control Room Supervisor position upon successful completion of the initial licensing class and SRO examination as detailed below. ** JOB SUMMARY/PURPOSE: The Control Room Supervisor ensures the safe and efficient operation of the plant, such that the health and safety of the workers and public are not jeopardized; give safety and quality a clear priority over schedule and cost; supervise the activities of the control room staff through all conditions of startup, shutdown power generation, and emergency. JOB DUTIES/RESPONSIBILITIES: Monitor and supervise plant operations in the Control Room. Coordinate with support staff to ensure that operations outside the Control Room are performed in a safe and controlled manner. Authorize and manage Control room access. Assure compliance with all applicable regulations and procedures. Authorize, in conjunction with the Shift Manager and support staff, the implementation of scheduled or unscheduled work activities affecting station equipment, including corrective and preventative maintenance, modifications and protective tagging. Utilize the station's corrective action programs to address issues and opportunities for improvement. Actively participate in training and personnel development opportunities. Participate in pre- and post-job evolutions. Assesses component operability/reportability. Communicate technical information, concepts, and ideas verbally and in writing. Serve as a member of the Emergency Response Organization. DUTIES/RESPONSIBILITIES AND REQUIREMENTS – INITIAL LICENSE TRAINING (ILT) CLASS: Complete training program to obtain NRC SRO License Self-regulated study time and field time, as necessary, to maintain ILT performance within standards Complete ILT Fundamentals, Systems and Simulator Phases Complete Simulator Certification Perform other job-related assignments and/or duties as assigned MINIMUM REQUIREMENTS Minimum education required of the position: B.S. Degree in Engineering or Sciences generally associated with nuclear power plant operations is highly desired, but not required if minimum experience requirements are met (NUREG 1021 and ACAD 00-003). Minimum experience required of the position: Held an ACTIVE RO or SRO license at a Pressurized Water Reactor (PWR) commercial nuclear power plant (same vendor and vintage) for at least 12 months OR Held an active Reactor Operator or Senior Reactor Operator license at a noncomparable commercial nuclear power plant for 18 months OR 18 months as a manager, supervisor, or staff member responsible for the coordination and implementation of plant equipment controls, integrated operations procedure, operations, maintenance, radiological support, modifications maintenance planning, work control, chemistry, or accredited training at a current or comparable (BWR/PWR) nuclear power plant OR At least 18 months as a qualified non-licensed operator; OR 24 months in a position equivalent to a reactor operator position at a military power reactor (qualified to manipulate or direct the manipulation of control rods) OR 48 months as a senior operator level certified instructor at a PWR commercial nuclear power plant (same vendor and vintage) OR 72 months as a senior operator level certified instructor at a non-comparable facility Note: Bullet items 3 and 4 require a B.S. Degree in addition to the requirements listed. Minimum knowledge, skills and abilities required of the position: Proficiency with current computer business applications. Outstanding communication and conflict management skills. Technical understanding of nuclear generation principles and operation. jobs.title: Supt, Fleet Refueling Services
jobs.description: Job Title: Supt, Fleet Refueling Services Work Place Flexibility: Onsite Legal Entity: Entergy Services, Inc.-ESI (OLD) Job Summary/Purpose Provides governance and oversight of Refueling Services readiness and implementation projects at Entergy Nuclear plants in accordance with regulatory requirements, company policies, and procedures. Job Duties/Responsibilities Ensure Fleet Refueling Services standards of conduct are executed and adhered to. Provide governance and oversight of site Sr, Project Managers and Supervisors for timely and quality completion of readiness/critique procedures. Attend Daily Fleet Outage Services/POD calls and report-out on applicable condition reports/corrective actions for respective sites. Attend all Refueling Services outage readiness meetings for respective sites/projects. Perform online and outage observations of Refueling Services leadership and ensure standards are being upheld. Promote Safe, Error-Free, Quality. Manage resources to support staffing of Refueling services online/pre-outage and outage scope, including Dry Fuel Storage. Review contracts, budget (online/outage), and load boards with the Site RF Project Manager and Finance IAW outage milestones and long-range plans. Review Vendor performance and assist in driving performance improvement initiatives for respective sites. Identify risks and gaps with Sr, Project Managers. Ensure adequate and timely actions are developed and completed in accordance with their associated due dates. Standardize processes, procedures, and equipment/tooling as applicable across respective site and facilitate change management through Sr, Project Managers. Attend Reactor Services industry working groups and owners’ groups meetings and provide best practices and lessons learned to stations to incorporate into outage readiness and execution. Develop and mentor Sr, Project Managers and Supervisors at respective site level. People leader for Fleet Outage Services. Single point of contact between station and Fleet for Fuel Services. Engage site stakeholders to build relationships and presence, and ensure alignment for support. Ensure Condition Reports are generated, screened, and tracked for Refueling Services GAPs, learnings, tooling/equipment issues, and performance issues. IF CAP product is required, THEN own the CR for your respective site or delegate to qualified Sr, Project Manager. Minimum Requirements Minimum education required of the position. B.S. degree in Engineering, Science, or a related discipline or equivalent experience. (Equivalent experience is defined as demonstrated success at the Supervisor or Sr, Project Manager level. A degree is very strongly preferred.) Minimum experience required of the position. 8+ years of experience in nuclear power design, construction, maintenance, operations, production, management or equivalent. Minimum knowledge, skills, and abilities required of the position. Technical proficiency in nuclear reactor services or project management. Technical understanding of nuclear generation principles and operation. Outstanding communication, conflict management, people leadership, and change management skills. Proficiency with current computer business applications. ANSI Required: Yes "ANSI/ANS-3. 1-1978 - 4.2.4 - 4.2.4 Technical Manager. For those nuclear power plants having a manager designated to supervise the on-site professional-technical groups, such managers, at the time of initial core loading or appointment of the position, whichever is later, shall have eight years in responsible positions related to power generation, of which three years shall be nuclear power plant experience. A maximum of four years of the remaining five years of experience may be fulfilled by satisfactory completion of academic or related technical training." Any certificates, licenses, etc. required of the position. Professional engineering license, SRO License or certification on a BWR or PWR (desired). ANSI Section 4.2.4 - Technical Manager. 8 years responsible positions related to power generation, of which 3 years shall be nuclear power plant experience. A maximum of 2 years of the remaining 6 years experience may be fulfilled by satisfactory completion of academic or related technical training. jobs.description: Work Place Flexibility: Onsite Legal Entity: Entergy Operations, Inc.-EOI **This position is based out of the Waterford 3 Nuclear facility in Killona, LA. This is an onsite role. Those outside of the area will be required to relocate and will have a minimum reporting timeframe. Relocation Assistance is not available for this posting*** *This role will be filled as a Spec-Nuc Safety/License II, III, or Licensing Spec, Sr (Nuc) based on education, relevant experience, and success at the licensing/regulator assurance level* JOB SUMMARY/PURPOSE:Responsible for the execution of the licensing processes and procedures at Entergy Nuclear. JOB DUTIES/RESPONSIBILITIES: Manages regulator's commitments. Interface with the NRC Resident inspectors and inspection teams. Maintains and implements regulations, including 10CFR50.59 and 10CFR72.48. Prepares, reviews and approves of correspondence to the NRC, including Licensee Event Reports, license amendments, allegation responses, generic letter and NRC Order responses, and required periodic reports. Submits performance indicator data to the NRC. Develops and revises procedures in accordance with current regulatory and administrative requirements. Coordinates SRC activities. Enhances communications by performing a liaison function between various work groups. Participates in the shared resources program. Serves as a member of the Emergency Response Organization. Interfaces with regulatory and industry representatives on behalf of station activities. Communicates technical information, concepts, and ideas verbally and in writing. jobs.title: IT Specialist I, II or Senior
jobs.description: Work Place Flexibility: Onsite Legal Entity: Entergy Operations, Inc.-EOI This is an onsite position in Port Gibson, MS. This position works 4/10's with every Friday off and the requirement is to work onsite every Monday-Thursday. Relocation assistance is not provided. Sponsorship is not provided. ***This position will be filled as an IT Specialist I, II or Senior depending on the candidate's qualifications and experience*** Position Purpose: This role develops and applies IT standards and procedures, as well as provide support for real-time and control systems. The consultant performs a wide variety of routine and nonroutine design and maintenance engineering activities, including but not limited to performance operations, planning, maintenance, computer applications, safety and root cause analysis. Primary Duties & Areas of Responsibility: Provides planning, guidance, and direction for the analysis, implementation, testing, debugging and documentation of cyber security controls for critical components and systems at the nuclear plants. This includes systems such as plant digital control systems, emergency planning systems, security systems and plant monitoring systems and components such as digital recorders, transmitters, feedback controllers, inverters/power supplies, relays, breakers, and process network infrastructure. Provides planning guidance, and operational support for the site cyber security infrastructure which includes such items as SIEMS, vulnerability and compliance scanners, networking components, servers and workstations. Plans and directs 24-hour system support of the cyber security program via establishment of callouts. Acts as a technical lead on the cyber security operations of the nuclear plant and the plant components identified as critical digital assets. Reviews the analysis of cyber security controls and recommends solutions to reduce the risk of cyber security sabotage to the nuclear plant and Entergy infrastructure. Oversees and directs the implementation of plans required to ensure nuclear sites adhere to the 10CFR73.54 cyber security regulations and ensure the sites meet the regulatory requirements. Plans and reviews periodic assessments, benchmarks and tabletops of the Cyber Security Program. Supports external parties in the audit and inspection of the Cyber Security Program. Provides input and commentary to the development and revision of procedures and policies relevant to the Cyber Security Program. Leads and reviews the analysis of incident response policies, takes an active role in cyber incident response and recovery, the identification of vulnerabilities in the network, and the performance of risk assessments on new and existing computer systems and equipment. Provides a technical resource on computer network and system security issues and technologies. Responsible for assuring that networking systems are monitored against cyber-attacks and risk mitigation counter measures are implemented to ensure the security of the organization. Develops and maintains a thorough knowledge of 10 CFR 73.54, NEI -08-09, site Cyber Security Plan, cyber security implementing procedures, and the cyber security defense-in-depth protective strategy. Resolves questions and issues on cyber security, CDA identification, and application of cyber security controls. Plans and monitors activities such as Critical Digital Asset (CDA) identification, design modifications involving CDA’s, control of portable digital media for connecting to CDAs, maintenance of CDA-relate documentation. Maintains compliance to Cyber Security Program implementing procedures. Participates in Emergency Response activities at the nuclear site (in addition to cyber emergency response). Minimum Educational Background and Physical Requirements Required to perform Job: Bachelor’s Degree in Computer Science or related discipline or experience equivalent Advanced Degree a plus Minimum Experience Required: IT Specialist Sr: 4+ years of relevant IT experience IT Consultant: 6+ years of relevant IT experience Experience with applying security to critical digital assets is a plus 3+ years' of solution design experience is a plus Experience at a nuclear site is a plus Experience with control systems security, security architecture, network security engineering or secure network development experience with electric power utilities is a plus Desired critical Knowledge, Skills, Abilities: Experience in implementing security control policy and procedures. Knowledge of the operation of electric power and/or generation systems. Knowledge of system hardening, patch management, and configuration management. Knowledge of security controls testing, security audits, and security assessments. Knowledge of fraud and computer forensics. Knowledge and understanding of NIST controls. Leadership Style/Characteristics: Highly motivated and ability to work with little direct supervision. Effective Teamwork and Inclusion (e.g. works collaboratively, builds collaborative relationships). Communicates openly, clearly and concisely, both verbally and in writing. Ability to act in a Leadership role and establish priorities, support and clear direction to subordinates #LI-TR1 #LI-ONSITEPrimary Location: Mississippi-Port Gibson Mississippi : Port Gibson Job Function: Nuclear FLSA Status: Professional Relocation Option: Level II Union description/code: NON BARGAINING UNIT Number of Openings: 1 Req ID: 119325 Travel Percentage:Up to 25% An Equal Opportunity Employer, Minority/Female/Disability/Vets. jobs.title: Engineer I - Sr, Fuels & Core Design (Nuc)
jobs.description: Posting End Date: Work Place Flexibility: Onsite Legal Entity: Entergy Services, Inc.-ESI (OLD) Job Summary/Purpose Core Design Engineers are responsible for engineering activities to provide safe, reliable, and predictable core designs, while improving the efficiency and robustness of our designs. In doing so, the position will support Entergy’s nuclear fleet in our mission to provide clean, reliable, and sustainable nuclear energy safely and efficiently. JOB DUTIES/RESPONSIBILITIES: Manage core reload projects including activities associated with the design and licensing of the core, fabrication and transportation of fuel, and contract management. Collaborate on fuel and core designs, including strategic fuel design evaluations, to lead the industry in advanced technologies for improved fuel performance, reliability, and cost effectiveness. Maintain detailed core models to support site reactor engineering and other plant functions in safely and reliably operating our nuclear fleet. Provide reactor physics and fuel criticality expertise to support the analytical and design bases on which each plant was licensed and provide technical oversight of contracted analyses. Employ robust designs to ensure that fuel is operated in a safe and reliable manner in accordance with plant design and licensing basis, within design limits, and with sustainable, failure-free performance. Support reload and startup activities by ensuring engineering work is performed with quality and predictability; programs are implemented per defined standards; fleet lessons learned are captured and discussed as part of continuous learning culture; and industry best practices are implemented. MINIMUM REQUIREMENTS: Minimum education required of the position: B.S. degree in Nuclear Engineering, or a closely related discipline (Physical Sciences) with equivalent experience. Desired: Master of Science degree in engineering or business administration Minimum experience required of the position: Engineer I: 0+ years of engineering Engineer II: 2+ years of experience (1year with a M.S.) Engineer III: 4+ years of experience Senior Engineer: 6+ years of experience (5+ years with PE) Desired: Experience in Reactor Engineering, Safety Analysis, Fuel Engineering, or other Engineering Discipline. Minimum knowledge, skills and abilities required of the position: Technical proficiency in nuclear fuel management. Technical understanding of nuclear generation principles and operation. Outstanding communication, conflict management, and change management skills. Proficiency with current computer business applications. Desired: Experience with nuclear design software packages (CASMO/SIMULATE, SCALE, MCNP, etc.). Desired: Experience with programming languages (Python, FORTRAN, Visual Basic, etc.) and shell scripting in UNIX/LINUX environments. Any certificates, licenses, etc. required for the position: Desired: Professional Engineering license (Nuclear discipline preferred). jobs.title: NDE Level II,  Spec III - Sr Lead
jobs.description: Job Title: NDE Level II, Spec III - Sr Lead Work Place Flexibility: Onsite Legal Entity: Entergy Services, Inc.-ESI (OLD) **This position can be filled as NDE Level II, Spec III - Sr Lead depending on experience.** Job Summary/Purpose Performs on-line NDE and assists Engineering program owners and planning for on-line and outage NDE. Interface with NDE Level IIIs for prioritizing and performing NDE fieldwork. Interface with site managers and/or implementing organizations requiring NDE to determine the scope of NDE required. Supports Work Planners by providing technical details for the planning and assembly of the necessary work packages to perform the required NDE. Support NDE scheduling by providing input to site Work Control organization. Status NDE during on-line and outage. Identifies potential NDE problems in advance and resolves support issues. Job Duties/Responsibilities Support Work Planners (On-line and Outage) by providing technical details for the planning and assembly of NDE packages. Status on-line and outage NDE activities in Plant Scheduling Programs. Support vendor in the field as required resolving any questions or concerns performing an examination. Resolve questions regarding support requirements (scaffold, insulation, preps, etc). Support Site by performing on-line NDE and maintaining the NDE/ISI database. Provide technical oversight of NDE contractors. Minimum Requirements Minimum education required of the position • High School graduate diploma or equivalent (GED). Minimum experience required of the position NDE Lv II certifications for a minimum of 3 years, with Nuclear experience strongly recommended. Minimum knowledge, skills, and abilities required of the position Must have the skills and ability to meet the qualification requirements set forth in the applicable codes and standards and possess or be able to obtain Level II certifications (if achieved, specify Level of qualification for qualified NDE methods including current PDI qualifications) in accordance with applicable Entergy procedures. Ability to use computer scheduling programs and be familiar with the tools and equipment Be able to perform inspections, examinations and tests in accordance with fleet procedures and work packages including natural or corrected near distance visual acuity of Snellen fraction 20/25 or better in at least one eye, uncorrected or corrected and ability to differentiate contrast between colors used in the NDE methods Have knowledge and skills to maintain the ISI/NDE database and nuclear plant scheduling programs Knowledge of other NDE-RT/UT/ET Have knowledge and skills to monitor outage schedule and make any necessary changes/updates as an outage progresses Be able to support on-line and outage NDE planning by providing technical details for NDE implementation and ensures schedule reflects available NDE resources Have the ability to resolve field implementation issues to keep NDE work on schedule. Any certificates, licenses, etc. required of the position Certified in various NDE techniques ? MT/PT/VT (Level II ASNT) Primary Location: Mississippi-Port Gibson Mississippi : Port Gibson Job Function: Nuclear FLSA Status: Professional Relocation Option: Level II Union description/code: NON BARGAINING UNIT Number of Openings: 1 Req ID: 122006 Travel Percentage:Up to 25% An Equal Opportunity Employer, Minority/Female/Disability/Vets. jobs.title: Engineer - Plant Support - WF3
jobs.description: Job Title: Engineer - Plant Support - WF3 Work Place Flexibility: Onsite Legal Entity: Entergy Operations, Inc.-EOI Job Summary/Purpose Identifies and resolves plant issues and acts as a liaison between engineering team and Operations and Maintenance departments. Job Duties/Responsibilities 1. Supports Maintenance and Operations in system troubleshooting and recommends course of action to more senior engineers for approval regarding troubleshooting significant events or complex plant problems. 2. Interfaces with Maintenance and Operations for cost effective maintenance strategies. Issues engineering changes (Design changes and temporary modifications) to support emergent plant issues and equipment reliability issues. 3. Helps support planning and prioritization aspects of project life cycle. 4. Implements action plans of senior team members to address deficiencies via the corrective action and work management processes. Supports changes in system configuration/design and design basis. 5. Develops engineering products in accordance with defined standards and procedures. Participates in project teams for critical on-line system outages or refueling outages. 6. Serves on outage engineering support teams. Attends training and maintains qualifications necessary to perform required group functions. Minimum Requirements Minimum education required of the position B.S. Degree in Electrical Engineering or other closely related scientific discipline/physical science generally associated with power plant operations or equivalent work experience (equivalent experience defined as demonstrated success at the engineer/tec Minimum experience required of the position 2 - 5 years experience in nuclear power design, operations or equivalent. Minimum knowledge, skills, and abilities required of the position Good to excellent communication skills. Physical ability to support plant walk downs and inspections. Experience/proficiency in PC use and engineering-specific applications. ANSI Required: N Any certificates, licenses, etc. required of the positionDesired: SRO license or certification on a PWR or BWR; Professional Engineering license. #LI-NW1Primary Location: Louisiana-Killona Louisiana : Killona Job Function: Nuclear FLSA Status: Professional Relocation Option: Level II Union description/code: NON BARGAINING UNIT-NBU Number of Openings: 1 Req ID: 121528 Travel Percentage:Up to 25% An Equal Opportunity Employer, Minority/Female/Disability/Vets. jobs.title: Electrical / I&C Engineer, Central Design
jobs.description: Job Title: Electrical / I&C Engineer, Central Design Work Place Flexibility: Onsite Legal Entity: Entergy Operations, Inc.-EOI Position is open to Killona, LA or Jackson, MS: relocation assistance available* JOB SUMMARY/PURPOSE Central Design Engineering provides high-quality, cost-effective, on schedule I&C design services for the Entergy Nuclear Fleet. Central Design Engineering is responsible for providing requested modification packages to the Nuclear Fleet. I&C modification design control is maintained through effective configuration management of design output documents (e.g., specifications, calculations, drawings, test requirements, and procedures). JOB DUTIES/RESPONSIBILITIES Prepare cost-effective modifications as assigned to improve plant performance and reliability. Prepare engineering products consistent with defined standards, procedures, and regulatory guidelines to ensure the site’s design and licensing basis is maintained. Assist in the solutions to issues that impact safe, reliable operations and propose process changes that improve overall efficiency. Provide technical support to operations, maintenance, engineering, and other fleet organizations. Manage I&C engineering work assignments to ensure the design service provided is meeting department and organizational needs. Communicate to all levels of the organization, verbally and in writing, technical information, concepts, and ideas throughout the engineering product development. Attend training and maintain the qualifications necessary to perform the required group functions. Evaluate the effect of radiated emission for both electromagnetic and radiofrequency emissions on plant equipment. Evaluate the susceptibility of plant components and devices to electromagnetic and radiofrequency emissions. Implement configuration controls for electrical systems and components. Conduct licensing activities such as 10CFR50.59 reviews, ensuring I&C electrical-specific considerations are applied. Coordinate electrical design activities with plant simulator personnel to ensure effects are reflected accurately on the plant reference simulator. Evaluate the effects of plant design changes on simulator operation, fidelity, configuration and certification. Determine preventive and predictive maintenance requirements for systems and equipment.Test and validate systems and components. Develop system and component life cycle strategies and plans. Develop I&C design inputs. Ability to manage multiple complex projects. MINIMUM REQUIREMENTS Minimum education required for the position B.S. degree in Electrical Engineering or another closely related scientific discipline/physical science generally associated with power plant operations or equivalent work experience (equivalent experience defined as demonstrated success at the engineer/technical specialist level.) Minimum experience required for the position Engineer, I: 0+ years of experience Engineer, II: 2+ years of experience (1+ year with master’s degree) Engineer, III: 4+ years of experience (3+ years with PE/master’s degree) Engineer, Senior: 6+ years of experience (5+ years with PE/master’s degree) Engineer, Senior Lead: 7+ years of experience (6+ years with PE/master’s degree) Minimum knowledge, skills, and abilities required for the position Nuclear Industry Engineering Change Packages qualification (IP-ENG-001) (Desired) Previous experience in Operations IT, Design or Systems engineering teams implementing engineering modifications. (Desired) Proficiency in computer codes that support Nuclear Design (e.g., ANSYS, ETAP, ME101, PipeFlo, MathCad, PDMS) (Desired) Any certificates, licenses, etc. required for the position Desired: Professional Engineering license and/or an SRO license or certification on a PWR or BWR. Primary Location: Louisiana-Killona Louisiana : Killona || Mississippi : Jackson Job Function: All Other Jobs FLSA Status: Professional Relocation Option: No Relocation Offered Union description/code: NON BARGAINING UNIT Number of Openings: 1 Req ID: 122066 Travel Percentage:Up to 25% An Equal Opportunity Employer, Minority/Female/Disability/Vets. jobs.title: Procurement Engineering Spec II - Sr.
jobs.description: Job Title: Procurement Engineering Spec II - Sr. Work Place Flexibility: Onsite Legal Entity: Entergy Operations, Inc.-EOI ***This position may be filled at the Procurement Engineering Spec, II, III, Sr or Sr Lead level depending on qualifications and experience of the selected candidate. *** Job Summary/Purpose: Responsible for the establishment of accurate technical and quality requirements, performance of Commercial Grade Dedication evaluations, and development of Item Equivalency Evaluations. Job Duties/Responsibilities: Developing Equivalency Evaluations to determine suitable replacement parts and components to address obsolete or unavailable items. Performing Parts Interchangeability Evaluations for replacement material, which is interchangeable with original plant equipment, with no change to form, fit, or function. Performing technical evaluations and reviews of procurement documents. Establishing and utilizing commercial grade dedications to support procurement activities. Evaluating and documenting the disposition of technical discrepancies identified during the procurement process, receipt inspection process, and inventory control process. Interfacing with internal and external groups on procurement related activities, as needed (e.g., supplier audit or Commercial Grade Survey checklists, establishing the technical and quality requirements of an item to be procured, interpretation of codes or standards, etc.). MINIMUM REQUIREMENTS: Minimum experience/education required of the position: Procurement Engineering Specialist II: Bachelor’s degree in Engineering or other scientific discipline, and minimum 1+ years of procurement engineering experience OR in lieu of a degree, minimum 5+ years of equivalent related experience. Procurement Engineering Specialist III: Bachelor’s degree in Engineering or other scientific discipline, and minimum 3+ years of procurement engineering experience OR in lieu of a degree, minimum 7+ years of equivalent related experience. Procurement Engineering Specialist Sr: Bachelor’s degree in Engineering or other scientific discipline, and minimum 6+ years of procurement engineering experience, with at least four (4) years supporting the nuclear energy industry OR in lieu of a degree, minimum 10+ years of equivalent related experience. Procurement Engineering Specialist Sr Lead: Bachelor’s degree in Engineering or other scientific discipline, and minimum 8+ years of procurement engineering experience, with at least six (6) years supporting the nuclear energy industry OR in lieu of a degree, minimum 12+ years of equivalent related experience Preferred experience for this position: Electrical experience or Electrical Engineering Degree is preferred for this position. Instrumentation & Controls experience. 2+ Years of Procurement Engineering experience. Technical mastery of engineering principles, in depth knowledge of procedures, programs, and standards in responsible areas. Performance and development of Commercial Grade Dedication evaluations. Minimum knowledge, skills and abilities required of the position: Knowledge of nuclear plant technology, systems and components. Ability to work with all disciplines (mechanical, electrical, instrumentation and controls), manufacturers and suppliers including contractors. Good problem solving and change-management skills. Ability to plan, organize and prioritize work for timely and accurate completion. Communicates technical information, concepts, and ideas verbally and in writing. Any certificates, licenses, etc. required of the position: Professional Engineering License and/or SRO License or Certification are helpful. jobs.title: Electrical Engineer I-Sr. (Nuclear)
jobs.description: Job Title: Electrical Engineer I-Sr. (Nuclear) Work Place Flexibility: Onsite Legal Entity: Entergy Operations, Inc.-EOI Job Summary/Purpose Responsible for providing technical leadership for the site through optimization and reliability of plant support systems and equipment, proactive identification and resolution of plant issues, and technical assistance to the Operations and Maintenance departments. Job Duties/Responsibilities1. System ownership/engagement/advocacy/prioritization for deficiency resolution leading to high levels of equipment reliability. Proactive performance monitoring, trending and system walk downs for assigned systems. 2. Use and sharing of Operating Experience (EN Fleet and industry). Work planning and prioritization (including long-range planning and life-cycle management). 3. Prioritize/establish action plans to address deficiencies on assigned systems via the corrective action and work management processes. Advocate appropriate outage work scope prioritization/PM implementation for improving system reliability. Interface with Maintenance and Operations for cost effective maintenance strategies on assigned systems. 4. Support Maintenance and Operations in system troubleshooting and provide system/discipline expertise for troubleshooting significant events or complex plant problems. 5. Support changes in assigned system configuration/design and design basis. Develop engineering products in accordance with defined standards and procedures. 6. Participate in project teams for critical on-line system outages or refueling outages. Serve on outage engineering support teams as assigned. Minimum Requirements Minimum education required of the positionB.S. Degree in Engineering or other closely related scientific discipline / physical science generally associated with power plant operations, or equivalent work experience (equivalent experience is defined as demonstrated success at the Engineer / Technician. Interpersonal SkillsExplains difficult or sensitive information; works to build consensus. jobs.description: Job Title: Mechanical Engineer I - Sr. (Nuc) Work Place Flexibility: Onsite Legal Entity: Entergy Operations, Inc.-EOI *This position may be filled as an Engineer II-Sr or Technical Specialist II-IV depending on qualifications and experience. * JOB SUMMARY/PURPOSEProvides technical support related to plant systems. Focuses on supporting day-to-day issues as well as maintaining related programs. JOB DUTIES/RESPONSIBILITIES Provides technical information concerning program performance requirements and bases, compliance status, and related industry operating experience to support site Engineering, Operations and Maintenance personnel. jobs.title: Technical Specialist - Fire Protection Engineer RBS
jobs.description: Job Title: Technical Specialist - Fire Protection Engineer RBS Work Place Flexibility: Onsite Legal Entity: Entergy Operations, Inc.-EOI Please note: Authorization to work in the United States is a precondition to employment in this position. Entergy will not sponsor candidates for work visas for this position. Note: This is an onsite role based out of the Riverbend Station Nuclear Facilitiy in St Francisville, LA. Note: The selected candidate will be placed as an Engineer I, II, III, or Senior OR Technical Specialist, I, II, III, or IV based on education and experience levels. If living outside the area, the selected candidate will need to relocate within 60 mins of the facility. Relocation assistance is avaialble for this posting. This position is for a Fire Protection Engineer. JOB SUMMARY/PURPOSE Conducts fire protection engineering activities and provides oversight of the site fire protection program as the Fire Protection Program Owner. Identifies and resolves plant issues and acts as a liaison between engineering team and Operations and Maintenance departments as the Fire Protection System Engineer. JOB DUTIES/RESPONSIBILITIES Support site with expertise in fire protection engineering principles, USNRC regulations (including Appendix R to 10CFR50, Safe Shutdown), Industrial Risk Insurance requirements, NYS Uniform Code and NFPA Code/Standards. Ensure physical plant and administrative changes do not adversely impact the fire protection program including the safe shutdown analysis or the Fire Probabilistic Risk Assessment. Perform fire hazard analyses, including fire modeling, to assess impact of proposed plant changes or identified deviations on the fire protection program. Ensure fire protection systems are maintained in accordance with requirements. Review surveillance and inspection results to ensure scope and frequency is optimized. Develop and present periodic reports to peer and station management regarding the overall health of the Fire Protection Program. Maintain fire protection program documents including the Design Basis Document, Safe Shutdown Analysis, Fire Hazard Analysis, and NFPA Code Conformance Review Report. Review proposed plant changes to ensure conformance with NFPA Codes/Standards, USNRC regulations, NYS Uniform Code, and fire risk insurance requirements. Provide oversight of the fire protection program as the Fire Protection Program Owner to ensure compliance with USNRC rules and regulations. Ensures the fire protection program is maintained such that the risk and consequences of fire is minimized. Actively participate in Entergy Fleet Peer groups and industry fire protection groups to ensure lessons learned and best practices are reviewed and implemented. Act as point of contact and lead for Fire Protection Program assessments and inspections. Develop, plan and coordinate special projects, corrective action plans, and improvement programs to address plant problems or regulatory issues. Controls engineering costs through the optimization of resources and tracking expenditures within budget parameters. Identifies and resolves plant issues and acts as a liaison between engineering team and Operations and Maintenance departments. Supports Maintenance and Operations in system troubleshooting and provides technical support regarding troubleshooting significant events or complex plant problems. Interfaces with Maintenance and Operations for cost effective maintenance strategies. Helps support planning and prioritization aspects of project life cycle. Implements action plans of senior team members and establishes/prioritizes actions plans to address deficiencies via the corrective action and work management processes. Supports changes in system configuration/design and design basis. Serves on outage engineering support teams. Attends training and maintains qualifications necessary to perform required group functions. Minimum Requirements Engineer: Minimum education required for the positionB.S. Degree in Engineering or another closely related scientific discipline/physical science generally associated with power plant operations or equivalent work experience (equivalent experience defined as demonstrated success at the engineer/technical spec level). Desired: Degree in Electrical or Mechanical Engineering Minimum experience required of the position• Engineer, I: 0+ years of experience• Engineer, II: 2+ years of experience• Engineer, III: 4+ years of experience• Senior Engineer: 6 years (5 years with PE) Technical Specialist: Minimum education required for the positionNone Minimum experience required of the position• Technical Specialist I: 0+ years commercial or military nuclear experience• Technical Specialist II: 2+ years commercial or military nuclear experience• Technical Specialist III: 4+ years commercial or military nuclear experience• Technical Specialist IV: 6+ years commercial or military nuclear experience Minimum knowledge, skills, and abilities required of the positionGood to excellent communication skills. Experience/proficiency in PC use and applications, depending upon the engineering level. Minimum knowledge, skills and abilities required of the position Proficient in fire protection engineering principles for Highly Protected Risk facilities including fire hazard analyses, fire modeling, fire performance tests, system failure analyses, Probabilistic Risk Assessment, NFPA Code conformance reviews, and model building code assessments. Proven ability to interface with Federal, State, and local regulatory agencies. Technical understanding of nuclear generation principles and operation is desired. Outstanding interpersonal, conflict management, problem solving, and change management skills. Must have strong written and oral communication skills. Must be skilled in the use of Microsoft desktop software. ANSI Required: No Any certificates, licenses, etc. required of the positionDesired: SRO license or certification on a PWR or BWR; Professional Engineering license. #LI-CC1 Primary Location: Louisiana-St. Francisville Louisiana : St. Francisville Job Function: Engineering FLSA Status: Professional Relocation Option: Level II Union description/code: NON BARGAINING UNIT Number of Openings: 1 Req ID: 121397 Travel Percentage:Up to 25% An Equal Opportunity Employer, Minority/Female/Disability/Vets. jobs.title: Senior Project Manager (TFE)
jobs.description: Job Title: Senior Project Manager (TFE) Work Place Flexibility: Hybrid Legal Entity: Entergy Services, Inc.-ESI (OLD) **This position can be filled in Entergy's service territories of Louisiana or Texas.** JOB SUMMARY/PURPOSE: Sr. Project Manager serves as the point of contact for all external generating and wholesale entities seeking transmission interconnection services. This position is additionally responsible for managing the transmission relationship of Entergy’s largest interconnection customers at both the retail and wholesale levels including reliability planning and ongoing maintenance coordination. This position develops and maintains various transmission agreements (e.g. LBA, MDMA, WDS, GIA, Revenue Sharing, System-toSystem Interconnections) with wholesale and neighboring systems to ensure that Transmission Services are delivered in a safe andreliable manner. JOB DUTIES/RESPONSIBILITIES: For Nuclear Power Plant Customers: Engage customers and establish routine multi-tier engagement processes for plant/grid and executive levels to understand service needs and discuss common issues. Support the development of transmission maintenance plans that meet customer service expectations. Support metrics to track service expectations. Develop routine review of all operating events impacting nuclear facilities and produce root cause, common cause, and corrective action plans where necessary. For Designated Industrial Customers and Other Special Accounts: Work cross functionally within Entergy and Transmission, develop strategic plans that serve industrial, nuclear, and other specially designated customers. Ensure customers’ expectations are understood and that maintenance and service levels align with customer needs. Advocate on behalf of customers within Transmission to ensure appropriate programs are in place and monitored. Support and execute customer engagement strategies with Entergy’s largest transmission customers. Develop specific customer vulnerability and action plans to ensure key account industrial reliability is met. Develop and maintain key customer agreements including Transmission interconnection agreements, Meter Data Management agreements, Local Balancing Area agreement, Generation Interconnection agreement, Facility Construction agreements, Facility Service agreements and Wholesale Distributions Service agreements. Lead the administration and interpretation of interconnection policies and procedures relative to specific Interconnection agreements. Provide support in regulatory proceedings on matters related to transmission service/interconnections. Provide leadership for the coordination and administration of future reimbursements for transmission facilities for customers such as Cooperative Energy, AECC, Arkansas Cities, CLECO, MDEA, MEAM, AEP, etc. Supports MISO Tariff-related activities applicable to the Entergy Operating Companies. MINIMUM REQUIREMENTS: Minimum Education:Bachelor's degree in electrical, mechanical, or civil engineering or equivalent work experience.Preferred: Bachelor's degree in Electrical Engineering. Minimum Experience:At least 7 years of progressively responsible experience in the utility industry. Preferred: Experience with Transmission and MISO tariffs. Experience in project management, engineering or construction. Protection & Control Knowledge; Experience with Large Industrial Customers; Experience with Large Data Centers Minimum Knowledge, Skills, and Abilities: Proficient in developing and reviewing contracts. Strong communicator both at tactical and strategic levels with experience discussing complex topics in a simple, easy-to understand way. Demonstrated teamwork skills and ability to execute projects in a matrix organization. Strong interpersonal skills with the ability to interact with customers at their CEO and General Manager level down to their operating personnel. Solid knowledge of Entergy transmission standards and practices. Any Certificates, Licenses, etc.: Preferred: PMP Certification.
